title: Support our first Global Voices Spotlight Issue: Human Perspectives on AI!
content:
This month we're exploring how the widespread adoption of AI is playing out for the Global Majority

Originally published on Global Voices

Image courtesy of Gabriela Mesones Rojo.

Every month, Global Voices will be choosing an urgent theme to explore in depth across all our regions. This range of perspectives — as always, grounded in our Global Voices values of human and digital rights — will be complemented by collaborative articles, online panels, and analysis of key narratives using our Civic Media Observatory methodology. title: Announcing Global Voices Spotlight!
content:
Each month we will be choosing an urgent theme to explore in depth across all our regions

Originally published on Global Voices

Image courtesy of Gabriela Mesones Rojo.

As the need for responsible, multi-perspective news coverage grows increasingly urgent, and the situation for media organizations and news providers is made increasingly difficult, Global Voices is responding by leaning into our strengths: our twenty years of building a worldwide community and centering the stories our contributors, translators, researchers, and fellows feel are important. title: AI porn isn’t regulated. What does that mean for depictions of queer bodies?
content:
What happens when you can create porn with artificial intelligence?

Originally published on Global Voices

By Emma Paidra

This story is part of Global Voices’ April 2026 Spotlight series, “Human perspectives on AI.” This series will offer insight into how AI is being used in global majority countries, how its use and implementation are affecting individual communities, what this AI experiment might mean for future generations, and more. You can support this coverage by donating here.
